name: add-atomic-chat-tool
tagline: Adds a local stdio MCP server so the container agent can call models served by the Atomic Chat desktop app over its local OpenAI-compatible.
verdict: Solid local-model integration: concrete copy/edit steps, real error handling, idempotency check, and no unsafe network, exec, secret, or privilege patterns found. Weak spots: no versioning/changelog signal, description doesn't state trigger phrasing, and the companion source files it claims to ship couldn't be verified to exist without a file inventory.

highlights.summary: Description states what it adds ('Add Atomic Chat MCP server...') but never phrases when/why to invoke it — no example trigger phrasings.
References bundled atomic-chat-mcp-stdio.ts, atomic-chat-env.ts and their tests as shipped in-folder, but no file inventory was available to confirm they exist.
No version field in frontmatter (lines 1-4) and no changelog or dated revision notes anywhere in the skill.
